## Summary

## Language

- [ ] Bahasa Indonesia (`README.id.md`)
- [ ] Español (`README.es.md`)
- [ ] 中文 (`README.zh.md`)
- [ ] 日本語 (`README.ja.md`)
- [ ] New language: `README.<code>.md`

## Source of truth

The English `README.md` is the source of truth. This PR should match its **structure** and **content**, not just be a literal sentence-for-sentence translation.

## Checklist

- [ ] The new/updated file is named `README.<code>.md` in the repo root
- [ ] I added a link to this translation in the language switcher of **every** existing `README*.md`
- [ ] Skill names, code, paths, and CLI flags are not translated
- [ ] Markdown heading levels and link structure match the English file
- [ ] I have read the file end-to-end in the target language and it makes sense
- [ ] If this is a brand-new language, the file follows the same template as the existing localized READMEs
